Single-letter values (G, M, L, A) are middle initials; 52.6% null typical for optional middle name
Values MD, DO, NP, PA-C, FNP, ANP are standard practitioner degree designations; enum=131 should cover these
Schema/token similarity+sample validation+semantic profile
Date of birth values in M/D/YY and M/D/YYYY formats; semantic profile confirms date type
SSN column; 100% null (likely redacted) but semantically maps directly
10-digit NPI identifiers for individual practitioners; this is the REQUIRED field
Primary taxonomy codes (207N00000X = Dermatology, 363A00000X = PA); first taxonomy is primary
Secondary taxonomy codes; maps to non-primary taxonomy field. 87% null consistent with optional secondary
Tertiary taxonomy; no dedicated third taxonomy field. 99% null. Mapped to groupSpecialtyTaxonomy as best available; may need custom handling
Contains combined name+specialty (e.g. 'Sam Awan, MD Dermatology'). Would need parsing to split into first/last name and specialty. Low confidence as single field cannot capture composite data
All values are 'Spec' indicating specialist designation; maps to specialist flag
Y/N hospital-based flag maps directly to isHospitalBased
Primary specialty names (Dermatology, Ophthalmology); first specialty is primary
Secondary specialty names (Mohs Surgery, Dermatopathology); maps to non-primary specialty
Tertiary specialty; no dedicated third specialty field. 99% null. Would duplicate target with Specialty 2; may need custom handling
Y/N flag for specialty inclusion in directory; closest match is network directory inclusion flag
Board certification names (AANP, NCCPA, American Board of Dermatology) map to primary board cert name
Board expiration dates; ~94% date type. Non-date values like 'Board Eligible' and 'Valid Indefinitely' will need special handling
Y/N flag for accepting patients maps directly; all values 'Y'
Min age restriction flag; all values 'N'. Field expects age value but source has Y/N indicator
Max age restriction flag; all values 'N'. Field expects age value but source has Y/N indicator
